# UnpopularVoice

> Financial teardowns of Indian startups from MCA filings. Revenue, PAT, debt and cash flow — audited numbers only. No press releases.

## About

UnpopularVoice publishes financial analysis of Indian startups built from MCA (Ministry of Corporate Affairs) annual filings and XBRL data. Each article extracts FY2025 revenue, PAT, operating cash flow, total debt, and employee costs directly from audited standalone filings. Data is sourced from AOC-4 XBRL submissions. No estimates, no analyst projections.

## Coverage

Indian startups across fintech, logistics, mobility, e-commerce, and consumer sectors. FY2025 filings (April 2024 - March 2025) are the primary dataset.

## Articles

- [Razorpay FY2025: Payments Business Profitable, ₹879 Cr Write-Down Drives Headline Loss](https://unpopularvoice.com/razorpay-fy2025)
- [PhonePe FY2025: Revenue ₹6,544 Cr, OCF +₹1,903 Cr, ₹2,017 Cr ESOP Charge](https://unpopularvoice.com/phonepe-fy2025)
- [Porter FY2025: Revenue ₹4,287 Cr, First Full-Year Profit +₹131 Cr, Zero Debt](https://unpopularvoice.com/porter-fy2025)

- [Infra.Market FY2025: Revenue ₹6,053 Cr (+37%), Net Profit ₹247 Cr | Profitable B2B Platform With Declining Margins](https://unpopularvoice.com/inframarket-fy2025)

- [Noise FY2025: Made ₹2 Cr Profit. Generated ₹176 Cr Cash. Revenue Fell 24%.](https://unpopularvoice.com/noise-fy2025)
- [UltraHuman FY2025: Made ₹106 Cr Profit. Revenue Grew 5x. Employee Costs Were ₹37 Cr.](https://unpopularvoice.com/ultrahuman-fy2025)

## Data Methodology

All financial figures are sourced from MCA AOC-4 XBRL filings. Values are in Indian Rupees Crores (1 Crore = 10 million). Standalone filings are used unless otherwise stated. FY2025 refers to the financial year ending March 31, 2025.
